title: hieracium hastile subsp. phlomoidiforme
intro: Hieracium hastile subsp. phlomoidiforme, also known as a species of hawkweed, is a plant that belongs to the dandelion family (Asteraceae) and is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and aiding in the treatment of skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Hieracium hastile subsp. phlomoidiforme can be prepared as an herbal infusion, tincture, or salve, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
